audit_items.proof_trace: In `interaction()`, the switch block explicitly guards `defaults` with `if (defaults)` checks (lines 117–127), confirming the function accepts NULL `defaults`. However, at lines 195–196, `ldap_charray_add(&defaults->resps, ...)` and `defaults->resps[defaults->nresps++]` are accessed without any null check — a NULL dereference if `defaults == NULL`. This path is reachable when `lutil_sasl_defaults()` returns NULL (OOM) and the unchecked return value is passed by callers (`common.c:1499`, `ldapvc.c:361`) to `lutil_sasl_interact()` with `LDAP_SASL_INTERACTIVE` flags.
audit_items.attack_scenario: Under memory pressure, `lutil_sasl_defaults()` returns NULL; callers in `common.c` and `ldapvc.c` pass this NULL directly as `defaults` to `ldap_sasl_interactive_bind()` with `LDAP_SASL_INTERACTIVE` flag; when the SASL library prompts and the user enters non-empty input, `interaction()` crashes at line 195 dereferencing NULL, causing a denial-of-service in the LDAP client or any server component using interactive SASL binding.
